scores.correctness_notes: Mostly correct but the scope field description is wrong — the response says 'K8s only — shares data only with the sidecar in the same pod', but the gold standard says scope is only relevant for machine charms (not Kubernetes), and container scope is used by subordinate charms requiring at least one container-scoped requires endpoint.
scores.hallucination_notes: The scope description ('K8s only — shares data only with the sidecar in the same pod') is factually inverted — container scope applies to machine charm subordinates, not Kubernetes. This is a minor inaccuracy rather than a fully invented parameter.
